steps.title: Subtract the equations
steps.content: The $3x$ terms match, so subtract the second equation from the first: $$(3x+6)-(3x+4)=4y-2y.$$

steps.title: Solve for $y$
steps.content: The left side simplifies to $2$, and the right side simplifies to $2y$, so $$2=2y.$$ Divide by $2$ to get $$y=1.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The question only asks for $y$, so there is no need to find $x$. The value of $y$ is 1.
